what_it_does: Renders the square/structure half of a wheel projection as an SVG inside a measured container. It draws a dyadic nested grid, the square outline, concentric inscribed circles, central axes, axis-circle coincidence points, a forbidden corner circle, and projection points placed at their dyadic-cell addresses. It also provides a separate transparent hit layer so users can select points.
observations: Uses a split pointer-events strategy: animated decorative dots have pointer-events:none, while larger transparent circles handle clicks.
Point positions are computed from dyadic cells and then clamped inside the square with a 4px pad, leaving the corner/forbidden zone empty.
A dashed circumscribed circle of radius sqrt(2)*R is drawn to mark the forbidden outside boundary.
Grid line weights come from dyadicLevels(proj.grid), producing coarser-to-fainter nested divisions.
Ring radii are linearly spaced from 0 to R based on proj.rings.
Coincidence points are rendered where each concentric circle intersects the horizontal and vertical axes.
Heavy conceptual comments frame the geometry as a dual between square (addressable) and circle (forbidden corner).
The component depends on useSize, stamp, gridCellCenter, dyadicLevels, sectorHue, pointAddress, and framer-motion.
standouts: Explicit separation of visual motion layer and invisible hit-test layer for interaction.
Clamping logic that enforces the addressable square vs forbidden corner narrative.
The dashed sqrt(2)*R corner circle as a deliberate, commented boundary marker.
conventions: React functional component with typed props interface.
SVG-first rendering with CSS custom properties for colors.
Custom hooks and utility imports for sizing, addressing, and theming.
AnimatePresence + motion.circle for enter/exit/selection animations keyed by seq.
concerns: place() and the hit layer recompute positions independently on every render.
Transparent hit circles have no aria labels or keyboard handling, reducing accessibility.
A 15px hit radius may extend beyond the clamped square boundary into the forbidden zone.
proj.points, proj.grid, proj.rings, and proj.n are assumed present without runtime validation beyond Math.max.
notes: Grid.tsx is the geometric scaffold of the wheel: it draws the square/circle dual, places addressable points, and delegates selection to a transparent SVG hit layer.
questions: What exact semantics do proj.grid, proj.rings, and proj.n encode?
How does gridCellCenter incorporate p.seq into the cell center calculation?
What is the intended user interaction when a point lies exactly on the clamped boundary?
Are the transparent hit circles meant to be keyboard-focusable or only mouse/touch targets?
What does stamp(addr) emit, and does it affect rendering or only tracing?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose (render the square/structure half of the seed with dyadic grid, axes, circles, forbidden corner, and points at their addresses) matches the rendered output.
apparent_themes: separation of visual representation and interaction
motion feel as a tokenized concern
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to make an abstract projection model visually legible: data points live at dyadic addresses inside a square, the circle represents an alternate coordinate system, and the area outside the square corners is explicitly marked as forbidden. It wants the geometry itself to carry meaning.
novelty: The interpretive, almost axiomatic comments that cast grid lines, axes, circles, and corners as a formal dual; the deliberate sqrt(2)*R forbidden corner circle; and the pointer-events split between animated decorative dots and invisible hit targets.
contribution: Provides the structural SVG rendering and point-placement logic for the wheel instrument, including the dyadic grid scaffolding, square/circle dual visualization, and a separate hit layer for selecting projection points.
summary_for_embedding: Grid.tsx is a React SVG component that draws a dyadic nested square grid, concentric inscribed circles, axes, coincidence points, and a forbidden corner circle, placing projection points at their dyadic cell addresses with a separate transparent hit layer for selection.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to render the structural square half of a wheel projection and enable point selection while keeping data inside an addressable box and visually demarcating the forbidden outside zone.

symbols.does: Renders the wheel's square/structure SVG grid, points, and selection hit layer.
symbols.description: Grid is the exported React component that takes a Projection, a MotionFeel, an optional selected sequence, and an onPick callback. It measures its container, computes the square and circle geometry, draws the dyadic grid and axes, renders projection points at their clamped dyadic-cell positions, and overlays transparent clickable circles so users can pick points.

symbols.does: Maps a 1-based ring index to its screen radius.
symbols.description: ringR is a small local function that returns (k / rings) * R, producing evenly spaced concentric circle radii from the center out to the square's half-edge R.

symbols.does: Computes and clamps a point's screen position inside the addressable square.
symbols.description: place uses gridCellCenter to derive a ProjPoint's screen coordinates from its dyadic cell (i, j, d), then clamps the result to a 4px-padded inset of the square. This keeps the rendered point inside the ratified/addressable region and away from the forbidden corner zone.
